point-1.heading: Not medical advice:
point-1.body: The AI assistant structures your input but does not make diagnoses or give medical recommendations.

point-2.heading: Data leaves your device:
point-2.body-prefix: When you use the AI assistant, your inputs are sent to Anthropic, via your own Anthropic account and with your own API key. Anthropic treats you as a direct customer, not Phylax. Anthropic stores requests and responses for 30 days for safety review and then deletes them automatically. Requests are not used for AI training. Phylax itself does not store chat histories on your device. Details on data handling by Anthropic:

point-3.heading: You control access:
point-3.body: Your API key is stored encrypted on your device. You can deactivate the AI function at any time and delete the key.

settings-section.error: AI settings could not be loaded.
settings-section.suspicious-warning: Key format looks unusual. Please check.

phylax-section.heading: What Phylax does
phylax-section.bullet-1: Does not store chat messages locally
phylax-section.bullet-2: Messages exist only during the session
phylax-section.bullet-3: Reloading the page deletes the chat

anthropic-section.heading: What Anthropic does
anthropic-section.bullet-1: Stores requests and responses for 30 days for safety review
anthropic-section.bullet-3: Does not use them for AI training

user-control-section.heading: What you control
user-control-section.bullet-1: The Anthropic account and API key are yours, not Phylax's
user-control-section.bullet-2: You see your usage directly in your Anthropic account
user-control-section.bullet-3: Deactivate the AI function in Settings at any time (deletes the key from Phylax)
user-control-section.bullet-5: Remove the chat at any time with "Clear"
user-control-section.bullet-6: Revoke the API key directly at Anthropic (takes effect immediately)

setup-wizard.title: Set up AI provider
setup-wizard.cors-blocked-warning: Note: this provider blocks direct browser requests. You can save the configuration, but calls will fail until Phylax supports a proxy or you configure your own CORS workaround.

step1.hint: Pick your AI provider. You can change the selection later in Settings.

step2.api-key-hint: Stored encrypted in the browser. Sent only to the chosen provider.
step2.no-key-hint: Local providers do not need an API key. Make sure your local server is running.

step3.hint: Test the connection to the selected provider.
